elements.sent-2411-072: A \tet{graphon is a measurable function $g: [0,1]^2 \to [0,1]$, with $g(y,x)=g(x,y)$ for every ordered pair $(x,y)$.}
elements.sent-2411-073: Two graphons $g_1$ and $g_2$ are said to be equivalent if there is a measure-preserving transformation $\sigma: [0,1] \to [0,1]$ such that $g_2(x,y) = g_1[\sigma(x), \sigma(y)]$ for almost every $(x,y)$.
elements.sent-2411-074: The equivalence classes of graphons are called reduced graphons.
elements.sent-2411-075: The Shannon entropy, edge density and triangle density of a graphon are given by the functionals \be S(g) = \iint H[g(x,y)] \, dx \, dy, \ee \be \edens(g) = \iint g(x,y) \, dx \, dy, \ee \be \tdens(g) = \iiint g(x,y) g(y,z) g(z,x) \, dx \, dy \, dz,\ee where \be H(u) = - [u \ln(u) + (1-u) \ln(1-u)] \ee is the usual entropy of independent coin flips with probability $u$ of getting heads.
elements.sent-2411-076: Note that if $g_1$ and $g_2$ are equivalent graphons, then $S(g_1)=S(g_2)$, $\edens(g_1)=\edens(g_2)$ and $\tdens(g_1)=\tdens(g_2)$.
